events.note: Writing Intent Check (revision 1) for the Atlas → Globalping migration scope

events.change_summary: Intent Check: full Atlas → Globalping replacement for zero-config in-network measurements

events.summary: Intent Check (revision 1): replace RIPE Atlas with Globalping as the sole in-network measurement backend, removing the API key/credit requirement for zero-config operation. Release/publishing and local-install cleanup are out of scope.

events.note: Exploring Atlas integration surface, then writing full scope as revision 2

events.change_summary: Full scope: replace RIPE Atlas backend with Globalping across 2 phases — new keyless backend module with tests, then CLI/display/docs rewiring and Atlas deletion

events.rationale: Backend swap with no UI work — terminal label and README text changes only, so design is skipped. Architecture is skipped because the new Globalping module is a one-for-one replacement of atlas.py inside an existing, well-understood boundary (discover, schedule, poll, parse) and the scope already carries detailed per-phase Files and Verification. Standard tier: about 7 files, builds entirely on existing module and error-handling patterns.

events.decision: Keep country.py's keyless Atlas probe-IP lookup (_get_atlas_probe_ip / RIPE_ATLAS_PROBES) despite deleting the Atlas measurement backend.
events.rationale: That lookup serves local measurements only — it finds a routable IPv4 per ASN to use as a traceroute target from the user's machine — and requires no API key. Globalping publishes no probe IP addresses, so there is nothing to migrate it to. The acceptance-criteria grep for "atlas" therefore excludes country.py, and tests/test_country.py stays untouched.

events.decision: Use Globalping's mtr measurement type (not traceroute) for the inside-out path from probes back to the user.
events.rationale: Globalping mtr results carry a per-hop ASN field, so the new module's AS-path parser needs no Cymru bulk lookup round-trip — unlike the Atlas parser, which had to resolve every hop IP via cymru_bulk_lookup_rich. Fewer network dependencies, simpler pure-function parsing, and the exact hop field names get verified against a live response in Phase 1.

events.decision: Remote in-network measurements run by default in country mode, gated by a new --no-remote opt-out flag instead of an API key.
events.rationale: The Atlas backend was implicitly opt-in via --atlas-key; with the key requirement gone, Globalping would silently become always-on, adding sweep time and consuming shared per-IP rate limits. A --no-remote flag (styled after --no-throughput) preserves a cheap escape hatch without reintroducing any configuration requirement. An optional --gp-token / NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN (mirroring --cf-token) raises the rate-limit tier for heavy users.

events.decision: Rename the atlas-profile command to coverage, and determine sweep coverage from a single GET /v1/probes inventory call instead of per-ASN queries.
events.rationale: The command name must not reference the old service (per approved intent), and "coverage" describes what it shows without binding the CLI surface to a vendor name. The single probe-inventory fetch returns every connected probe with ASN and country metadata, so one request powers both the pre-sweep "probes found in X/Y ASNs" report and the per-country coverage table — replacing N per-ASN Atlas queries plus the paginated coverage crawl. The anchors column disappears; Globalping has no anchor class (accepted trade-off).

events.summary: Full scope pushed as revision 2. Two phases: (1) build the new Globalping backend module with unit tests, (2) switch cli.py/display.py/globe.py/README over and delete atlas.py and its tests. 13 requirements, 9 acceptance criteria. Key calls: mtr measurement type gives per-hop ASN so the remote parser drops the Cymru lookup; remote measurements default-on with a --no-remote opt-out and optional --gp-token for higher rate limits; atlas-profile becomes coverage; country.py's keyless Atlas probe-IP oracle for local test-IP discovery stays (Globalping publishes no probe IPs). Workflow classified backend-feature/standard, skipping design and architecture. Four decisions logged.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting phase 1: building the Globalping backend module and its unit tests

events.description: Baseline clean (75 passed, ruff ok); Globalping schema verified against live spec; implementing globalping.py
Module and tests written; running verification checks

events.details: Full suite: 101 passed in 0.10s (75 pre-existing + 26 new in tests/test_globalping.py). ruff check src tests: clean. Module imports in isolation. Baseline before changes was 75 passed with no pre-existing failures.

alternatives_considered: Bare AS numbers only (loses all readability the Atlas labels had)
Cymru bulk lookup for AS names (explicitly dropped by scope)
Full resolvedHostname in the label (too noisy for the path line)
events.decision: Implementation: Globalping mtr hops carry no network-name field, so AS-path labels use the registered domain from each hop's resolvedHostname ("AS174 (cogentco.com)"), falling back to bare "AS{n}".
events.rationale: Verified against the live OpenAPI spec (api.globalping.io/v1/spec.yaml): each mtr hop carries only resolvedAddress, resolvedHostname, asn (array of integers), stats, and timings — there is no network/organisation name. Reintroducing a Cymru lookup for names would defeat the scope's stated goal of dropping that round-trip, so a small pure _hostname_domain() heuristic (last two hostname labels) supplies a readable name where a hostname exists. Also confirmed live: ping stats live at result.stats.{min,avg,max}, results items are {probe, result}, measurement status values are in-progress/finished, 422 = no matching probes, 429 = rate limit.

events.decision: Implementation: schedule_measurements() raises requests.HTTPError (with the response attached) for 422/429/401 rather than defining a custom exception type.
events.rationale: Mirrors atlas.schedule_measurements' contract, so the Phase 2 cli.py wiring can catch HTTPError and branch on e.response.status_code to produce the per-ASN probe_errors messages the edge cases require (no coverage / rate limit with --gp-token hint / invalid token). All other fetchers (fetch_probes, fetch_results, get_public_ip) keep the Atlas convention of swallowing errors and returning an empty value, since the sweep must continue past per-ASN failures.

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: Build the Globalping backend module

events.summary: Built the complete Globalping backend module (src/netpath/globalping.py): probe inventory via a single GET /v1/probes, client-side ASN and country coverage counting, ping+mtr measurement scheduling with magic AS-locations and limit 3, 2s/60s polling with timed_out marking, and pure result parsers (aggregated ping RTT; deduplicated AS-hop path from per-hop asn fields with hostname-domain names — no Cymru lookup). Optional Bearer token supported on every call, never required. Result schemas verified against the live Globalping OpenAPI spec before locking the parsers. 26 new mocked-requests tests; full suite 101 passed, ruff clean. No existing files touched.

items.description: Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_globalping.py -q` and confirm all 26 tests pass
Run `.venv/bin/python -c "from netpath import globalping; print(len(globalping.fetch_probes()))"` with no token or env var set and confirm it prints a positive probe count from the live API

Run `.venv/bin/python -c "from netpath import globalping as gp; c = gp.coverage_by_country(gp.fetch_probes()); print(sorted(c.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:5])"` and confirm a plausible top-5 country list (e.g. `DE`, `US` near the top)

Live end-to-end check against a well-covered network: run `.venv/bin/python -c "from netpath import globalping as gp; m = gp.schedule_measurements('AS15169', '8.8.8.8', gp.get_public_ip()); s = gp.poll_until_done(list(m.values())); print(s); print(gp.parse_ping_rtt(gp.fetch_results(m['ping']))); print(gp.parse_mtr_as_path(gp.fetch_results(m['mtr'])))"` and confirm finished statuses, a min/avg/max RTT dict, and an AS-path list like `['AS15169', 'AS174 (cogentco.com)', ...]` within about 30 seconds
Run `.venv/bin/python -m ruff check src tests` and confirm it reports no issues

Confirm no existing behavior changed: `git status` shows only the two new files `src/netpath/globalping.py` and `tests/test_globalping.py` (plus task metadata), and `netpath country --help` still shows `--atlas-key` (the switch-over happens in Phase 2)

events.note: Starting phase 2: switching cli/display/globe/README to Globalping and deleting the Atlas backend

events.description: Starting phase 2: Switch the CLI over and delete Atlas
Implementing core changes: rewiring cli.py to Globalping, updating display/globe, rewriting README
Full check suite passed; recording results

events.details: Full suite: 96 passed in 0.07s (baseline this phase was 101; the 5 tests in the deleted tests/test_atlas.py account for the drop). ruff check src tests: clean. grep -ri atlas over cli.py/display.py/globe.py/README.md: no matches. Live smoke: netpath coverage --top 5 returned a ranked probe table with no token set; country --help shows --gp-token and --no-remote, no --atlas-key.

alternatives_considered: Name NETPATH_ATLAS_KEY literally in the note (fails the acceptance grep)
Drop the migration note entirely (loses the upgrade guidance the edge case asks for)
events.decision: Implementation: the README migration note avoids the literal word "Atlas" (and the NETPATH_ATLAS_KEY variable name) to satisfy the acceptance-criteria grep; it describes the removed backend as "a backend that required an API key and a credit balance" and tells users the old key flag and environment variable are obsolete and silently ignored.
events.rationale: Two task requirements conflict literally: the acceptance criterion (repeated in Phase 2 verification) requires `grep -ri atlas README.md` to return no matches, while the stale-env-var edge case asks the README migration note to name the obsolete variable, which necessarily contains the string ATLAS. The grep criterion is the mechanically verified one and appears twice, so it wins; the migration note still fulfils its intent — telling upgrading users that the old key flag and env var are gone and ignorable — without the literal string.

events.change_summary: Phase 2 complete: Switch the CLI over and delete Atlas

events.summary: Switched the CLI to Globalping and deleted the Atlas backend. country now runs remote in-network measurements by default with no key: one probe-inventory request determines coverage, ping+mtr are scheduled per covered ASN, results merge under a "globalping" row key, and --no-remote opts out; --gp-token / NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN optionally raises the rate limit. 422/429/401 map to per-ASN probe_errors entries (the 429 message points at --gp-token; 401 surfaces a clear token error without aborting the sweep). atlas-profile is now the zero-config coverage command with a probes-only table. display shows a [Globalping] subrow (anchor variant removed), the globe choropleth is retitled, and the README's Atlas section is replaced by a Globalping section plus an upgrade note. atlas.py and test_atlas.py deleted; grep -ri atlas over cli/display/globe/README is empty; 96 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: In a clean environment (no `NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN` set, stale `NETPATH_ATLAS_KEY` may remain and must be silently ignored), run `.venv/bin/netpath country ZA --top 5 --no-throughput` and confirm you see "Discovering Globalping probes" and either a `[Globalping]` subrow under covered ISPs in the summary or a per-ASN "no Globalping coverage" partial-results note in the diagnosis panel

Run `.venv/bin/netpath coverage --top 10` with no token set and confirm a ranked per-country probe table appears (US and DE near the top), then run `.venv/bin/netpath coverage --top 20 --globe` and confirm the browser opens a choropleth titled "Globalping Coverage by Country" with a probes-only colorbar
Run `.venv/bin/netpath country ZA --top 5 --no-throughput --no-remote` and confirm no Globalping status lines appear anywhere in the output

Run `.venv/bin/netpath --help`, `.venv/bin/netpath country --help`, and `.venv/bin/netpath coverage --help` and confirm none of them mention Atlas, a required API key, or credits, and that `country --help` lists `--gp-token` and `--no-remote` but no `--atlas-key`

Run `grep -ri atlas src/netpath/cli.py src/netpath/display.py src/netpath/globe.py README.md` and confirm it prints nothing, and confirm `src/netpath/atlas.py` and `tests/test_atlas.py` no longer exist
Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` and `.venv/bin/python -m ruff check src tests` and confirm 96 tests pass and lint is clean

Read the "Upgrading from earlier versions" section in `README.md` and confirm the migration guidance is acceptable given it deliberately avoids the literal old service and env-var names to keep the acceptance grep clean (decision logged this phase)

workflow.rationale: Backend swap with no UI work — terminal label and README text changes only, so design is skipped. Architecture is skipped because the new Globalping module is a one-for-one replacement of atlas.py inside an existing, well-understood boundary (discover, schedule, poll, parse) and the scope already carries detailed per-phase Files and Verification. Standard tier: about 7 files, builds entirely on existing module and error-handling patterns.

stageContent.release:
## Release Notes

### What was built
- Replaced the RIPE Atlas backend with Globalping as the sole in-network measurement backend, eliminating the API key, account, and credit-balance prerequisite
- New `src/netpath/globalping.py` module: probe inventory via a single `GET /v1/probes`, ping and mtr measurement scheduling, 2 s/60 s polling, and pure result parsers (aggregated RTT from ping; deduplicated AS-hop path from per-hop `asn` fields — no Cymru lookup)
- Country mode now runs in-network measurements by default; `--no-remote` opts out; optional `--gp-token` / `NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN` raises the rate-limit tier
- `atlas-profile` command renamed to `coverage`; zero-config, no key-check, probes-only table
- `atlas.py` and `tests/test_atlas.py` deleted; 26-test `tests/test_globalping.py` added

### Key decisions
- AS-path labels use the registered domain from the hop's `resolvedHostname` ("AS174 (cogentco.com)") since Globalping mtr hops carry no network-name field — Cymru lookup dropped from remote path parsing
- `schedule_measurements()` raises `requests.HTTPError` (with response attached) for 422/429/401 so `cli.py` can branch on `status_code` for per-ASN `probe_errors` messages
- README migration note avoids the literal word "Atlas" to satisfy the acceptance grep while still communicating upgrade guidance
- Single `GET /v1/probes` inventory request aggregated client-side for both pre-sweep coverage check and the `coverage` command — no per-ASN queries, no budget concept
- Remote measurements default-on in country mode; `--no-remote` flag (styled after `--no-throughput`) provides opt-out without reintroducing any key requirement

### Changes by phase
- **Phase 1: Build the Globalping backend module** — Created `src/netpath/globalping.py` with full integration (probe inventory, scheduling, polling, parsers) and `tests/test_globalping.py` with 26 mocked-requests tests; verified against the live Globalping OpenAPI spec; full suite 101 passed, ruff clean; no existing files touched
- **Phase 2: Switch the CLI over and delete Atlas** — Rewired `cli.py` to Globalping (drops `--atlas-key`, adds `--gp-token`/`--no-remote`, renames `atlas-profile` to `coverage`); updated `display.py` ([Globalping] subrow, anchor variant removed), `globe.py` (choropleth retitled), `README.md` (Atlas section replaced by Globalping section plus upgrade note); deleted `atlas.py` and `test_atlas.py`; acceptance grep returns nothing; 96 tests pass, ruff clean; live `netpath coverage --top 5` smoke test confirmed zero-config operation

## Verification

### Production Build
PASS — `python -m build` produced `netpath-0.10.1.dev16+g298be4378.tar.gz` and `netpath-0.10.1.dev16+g298be4378-py3-none-any.whl`

### Review Reference
Approved by reviewer on 2026-07-02 — 9/9 acceptance criteria, pytest 96 passed, ruff clean
stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Build the Globalping backend module — complete

**Files created:**
- `src/netpath/globalping.py` — the complete Globalping integration: `get_public_ip()` (carried over from atlas.py unchanged), `fetch_probes()` (single GET /v1/probes inventory), `count_probes_by_asn()` and `coverage_by_country()` (pure client-side aggregation over the inventory, satisfying R2's one-request coverage model), `schedule_measurements()` (POST /v1/measurements: ping to the per-ASN test IP plus mtr back to the user's public IP, `locations: [{"magic": "AS{n}"}]`, `limit: 3`), `poll_until_done()` (2 s interval, 60 s default timeout, unfinished measurements marked "timed_out"), `fetch_results()`, `parse_ping_rtt()` (min-of-mins / mean-of-avgs / max-of-maxes across probes), and `parse_mtr_as_path()` (consecutive-ASN dedup using the per-hop `asn` field — no Cymru lookup).
- `tests/test_globalping.py` — 26 tests in the same mock-`requests` style as the deleted-in-phase-2 test_atlas.py: inventory fetch and error paths, Bearer-header injection and its absence without a token (acceptance-criterion test), ASN/country coverage counting, ping+mtr scheduling body shape, AS-prefix normalisation, 422 propagation, poll terminal/timeout behavior, RTT aggregation with null-stats (100 % loss) handling, mtr path dedup/labeling/fall-through, and ipify parsing.

**Schema verification (required by the phase before locking parsers):** confirmed against the live OpenAPI spec at api.globalping.io/v1/spec.yaml — mtr hops carry `asn` as an array of integers plus `resolvedHostname` (no network-name field), ping stats live at `result.stats.{min,avg,max}`, result items are `{probe, result}`, top-level status is `in-progress`/`finished`, 422 means no matching probes, 429 rate limit.

**Deviations/refinements:** AS-path labels take their readable name from the hop hostname's registered domain ("AS174 (cogentco.com)") since Globalping provides no AS organisation names (decision logged). Errors from measurement creation propagate as requests.HTTPError with the response attached so Phase 2 can branch on 422/429/401 (decision logged).

**Checks:** full suite 101 passed (baseline was 75, no pre-existing failures), `ruff check src tests` clean, `python -c "from netpath import globalping"` imports cleanly. No existing files touched — no user-visible change yet.

## Phase 2: Switch the CLI over and delete Atlas — complete

**Files changed:**
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — Atlas import replaced with `globalping as globalping_mod` (plus `requests` for HTTPError branching). `_set_atlas_error` is now `_set_globalping_error` writing the `globalping` probe_errors key. The `country` command drops `--atlas-key`/`NETPATH_ATLAS_KEY` and gains `--gp-token` (envvar `NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN`, shared `_GP_TOK` option in the `--cf-token` style) and `--no-remote` (styled after `--no-throughput`). Pre-sweep discovery is one `fetch_probes()` inventory call aggregated client-side (no per-ASN queries, no budget check). Post-sweep block schedules ping+mtr per covered ASN, polls with the 2 s/60 s cadence, and merges results under a `globalping` row key; HTTPError branches: 422 → "no Globalping coverage", 429 → "rate limit reached — pass --gp-token for higher limits", 401 → clear error naming the token, remaining ASNs marked "invalid Globalping token", scheduling stops but the sweep completes. `atlas-profile` is now `coverage`: zero-config, no key-check exit, probes-only table (no anchors column), `--top`/`--globe` unchanged.
- `src/netpath/display.py` — `_render_atlas_subrow` is now `_render_globalping_subrow`: reads the `globalping` key, prints a `[Globalping]` tag; the `[Atlas anchor]` variant and `source` handling are gone.
- `src/netpath/globe.py` — choropleth titled "Globalping Coverage by Country"; colorbar/hover say "Probes" (no anchors); `render_coverage` interface unchanged.
- `README.md` — country options updated (`--no-remote`, `--gp-token`), "Probe coverage" section for `netpath coverage`, and the RIPE Atlas section replaced by a "Globalping" section (zero-config default, `[Globalping]` output example, optional-token subsection, `netpath coverage` usage) plus an "Upgrading from earlier versions" note.
- Deleted: `src/netpath/atlas.py`, `tests/test_atlas.py`.

**Deviation (decision logged):** the acceptance grep (`grep -ri atlas … README.md` must be empty) and the stale-env-var edge case (README note naming `NETPATH_ATLAS_KEY`) conflict literally. The grep criterion wins; the migration note describes the removed key-and-credits backend and says the old flag/env var are obsolete and silently ignored, without the literal string.

**Checks:** full suite 96 passed (101 minus the 5 deleted Atlas tests), `ruff check src tests` clean, acceptance grep returns nothing, `netpath country --help` shows `--gp-token`/`--no-remote` with no Atlas/credit mention, `netpath coverage --help` works, and a live `netpath coverage --top 5` smoke test returned a ranked probe table with no token set.
stageContent.review:
## Verdict

**Verdict:** APPROVE
**Files reviewed:** 8 files changed across 2 phases

All 9 acceptance criteria pass: in-network measurements run without any key, the coverage command works zero-config, the Atlas backend and its tests are fully deleted with no grep survivors, and the new test suite covers all required parse and inventory paths. Automated checks (96 tests, ruff) are clean.

### Automated Checks

| Check | Result | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Test suite (pytest) | PASS | 96 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped |
| Lint (ruff) | PASS | All checks passed |

### Acceptance Criteria (9/9 passed)

- [x] AC-1: netpath country ZA --top 5 performs in-network measurements with no token, key, or environment variable set; verified by running the command in a clean environment and observing a [Globalping] subrow or a per-ASN "no Globalping coverage" note — PASS: cli.py:579-580 gp_token defaults None, no_remote defaults False; cli.py:636-656 probe inventory fetched without token; cli.py:813 result merged under "globalping" row key; display.py:385-400 renders [Globalping] subrow
- [x] AC-2: netpath coverage --top 10 prints a ranked per-country probe table with zero configuration, and --globe renders the choropleth — PASS: cli.py:870-908: coverage command; gp_token defaults None (cli.py:872); ranked table printed at cli.py:885-903; globe_mod.render_coverage called at cli.py:908; netpath coverage --help confirmed zero-config
- [x] AC-3: --gp-token and NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN are accepted on the country and coverage commands and produce an Authorization: Bearer header; verified by a unit test asserting the header in tests/test_globalping.py — PASS: cli.py:36-38: _GP_TOK option with envvar=NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN; both commands use _GP_TOK (cli.py:579,872); tests/test_globalping.py:38-55 (Bearer with token, no header without); tests/test_globalping.py:120-128 (schedule_measurements Bearer)
- [x] AC-4: --no-remote on the country command skips all Globalping activity; verified by running with the flag and observing no Globalping status lines — PASS: cli.py:636,680,703,731: all Globalping blocks gated by `if not no_remote:`; cli.py:580 no_remote declared with --no-remote flag; netpath country --help confirms flag present
- [x] AC-5: src/netpath/atlas.py and tests/test_atlas.py no longer exist, and grep -ri atlas src/netpath/cli.py src/netpath/display.py src/netpath/globe.py README.md returns no matches — PASS: ls src/netpath/ shows no atlas.py; ls tests/ shows no test_atlas.py; grep -in atlas across all four target files returned no output
- [x] AC-6: netpath --help, netpath country --help, and netpath coverage --help contain no mention of Atlas, API keys as a requirement, or credits — PASS: netpath --help: lists coverage not atlas-profile; country --help: shows --gp-token and --no-remote, no --atlas-key, no credits; coverage --help: zero-config help text confirmed
- [x] AC-7: JSON output for country mode uses a globalping key for remote results and contains no atlas key or atlas_anchor source tag; verified by inspecting --json output or the MeasurementResult-adjacent row shape — PASS: cli.py:813: _row["globalping"] = _gp_data; cli.py:47: probe_errors["globalping"]; grep for atlas/atlas_anchor keys in cli.py/display.py returns no output; country command has no --json flag so row shape is the applicable criterion
- [x] AC-8: tests/test_globalping.py covers ping RTT parsing, mtr AS-path parsing, coverage counting, and error/empty responses; pytest passes — PASS: tests/test_globalping.py: parse_ping_rtt (lines 186-212), parse_mtr_as_path (lines 221-268), count_probes_by_asn + coverage_by_country (lines 60-84), error paths (lines 29-35, 175-181, 206-212); pytest: 96 passed
- [x] AC-9: ruff check src tests passes — PASS: ruff check src tests: All checks passed

### Code Quality (Refactor Review)

No code quality issues found in changed files.

### Security Assessment (Security Review)

No security issues found in changed files.

### Decisions Made During Implementation

- AS-path labels derived from hop hostname registered domain ("AS174 (cogentco.com)") since Globalping mtr hops carry no network-name field — Cymru lookup dropped from remote path parsing
- schedule_measurements() raises requests.HTTPError for 422/429/401 (response attached) so cli.py can branch on status_code for per-ASN error messages
- README migration note avoids the literal word "Atlas" to satisfy the acceptance grep while still communicating upgrade guidance
- Single GET /v1/probes inventory request aggregated client-side for both pre-sweep coverage check and the coverage command — no per-ASN queries, no budget concept
- Remote measurements default-on in country mode; --no-remote flag (styled after --no-throughput) provides opt-out without reintroducing any key requirement

## Required Changes

None.

reviewPayload.summary: All 9 acceptance criteria pass: in-network measurements run without any key, the coverage command works zero-config, the Atlas backend and its tests are fully deleted with no grep survivors, and the new test suite covers all required parse and inventory paths. Automated checks (96 tests, ruff) are clean.

acceptanceCriteria.text: netpath country ZA --top 5 performs in-network measurements with no token, key, or environment variable set; verified by running the command in a clean environment and observing a [Globalping] subrow or a per-ASN "no Globalping coverage" note
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: cli.py:579-580 gp_token defaults None, no_remote defaults False; cli.py:636-656 probe inventory fetched without token; cli.py:813 result merged under "globalping" row key; display.py:385-400 renders [Globalping] subrow

acceptanceCriteria.text: netpath coverage --top 10 prints a ranked per-country probe table with zero configuration, and --globe renders the choropleth
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: cli.py:870-908: coverage command; gp_token defaults None (cli.py:872); ranked table printed at cli.py:885-903; globe_mod.render_coverage called at cli.py:908; netpath coverage --help confirmed zero-config

acceptanceCriteria.text: --gp-token and NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN are accepted on the country and coverage commands and produce an Authorization: Bearer header; verified by a unit test asserting the header in tests/test_globalping.py
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: cli.py:36-38: _GP_TOK option with envvar=NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN; both commands use _GP_TOK (cli.py:579,872); tests/test_globalping.py:38-55 (Bearer with token, no header without); tests/test_globalping.py:120-128 (schedule_measurements Bearer)

acceptanceCriteria.text: --no-remote on the country command skips all Globalping activity; verified by running with the flag and observing no Globalping status lines
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: cli.py:636,680,703,731: all Globalping blocks gated by `if not no_remote:`; cli.py:580 no_remote declared with --no-remote flag; netpath country --help confirms flag present

acceptanceCriteria.text: src/netpath/atlas.py and tests/test_atlas.py no longer exist, and grep -ri atlas src/netpath/cli.py src/netpath/display.py src/netpath/globe.py README.md returns no matches
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: ls src/netpath/ shows no atlas.py; ls tests/ shows no test_atlas.py; grep -in atlas across all four target files returned no output

acceptanceCriteria.text: netpath --help, netpath country --help, and netpath coverage --help contain no mention of Atlas, API keys as a requirement, or credits
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: netpath --help: lists coverage not atlas-profile; country --help: shows --gp-token and --no-remote, no --atlas-key, no credits; coverage --help: zero-config help text confirmed

acceptanceCriteria.text: JSON output for country mode uses a globalping key for remote results and contains no atlas key or atlas_anchor source tag; verified by inspecting --json output or the MeasurementResult-adjacent row shape
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: cli.py:813: _row["globalping"] = _gp_data; cli.py:47: probe_errors["globalping"]; grep for atlas/atlas_anchor keys in cli.py/display.py returns no output; country command has no --json flag so row shape is the applicable criterion

acceptanceCriteria.text: tests/test_globalping.py covers ping RTT parsing, mtr AS-path parsing, coverage counting, and error/empty responses; pytest passes
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: tests/test_globalping.py: parse_ping_rtt (lines 186-212), parse_mtr_as_path (lines 221-268), count_probes_by_asn + coverage_by_country (lines 60-84), error paths (lines 29-35, 175-181, 206-212); pytest: 96 passed
ruff check src tests: All checks passed

decisions: AS-path labels derived from hop hostname registered domain ("AS174 (cogentco.com)") since Globalping mtr hops carry no network-name field — Cymru lookup dropped from remote path parsing
schedule_measurements() raises requests.HTTPError for 422/429/401 (response attached) so cli.py can branch on status_code for per-ASN error messages
README migration note avoids the literal word "Atlas" to satisfy the acceptance grep while still communicating upgrade guidance
Single GET /v1/probes inventory request aggregated client-side for both pre-sweep coverage check and the coverage command — no per-ASN queries, no budget concept
Remote measurements default-on in country mode; --no-remote flag (styled after --no-throughput) provides opt-out without reintroducing any key requirement

changeAnnotations.note: New single-purpose module: probe inventory, measurement scheduling, polling, and result parsing for the Globalping API — replaces atlas.py
26 mocked-requests unit tests covering the new globalping module's public API surface, including Bearer header injection and error paths
Replaces Atlas import and all wiring with Globalping: drops --atlas-key, adds --gp-token and --no-remote, renames atlas-profile to coverage, rewires pre/post-sweep blocks
Renames _render_atlas_subrow to _render_globalping_subrow: reads the globalping row key, prints [Globalping] tag, removes the [Atlas anchor] variant
Updates choropleth title to "Globalping Coverage by Country" and colorbar/hover to "Probes"; render_coverage interface unchanged
Replaces the RIPE Atlas section with a Globalping section (zero-config, optional token, coverage command) and adds an upgrading-from-earlier-versions note
